## Discussion

To match on only one criterion, add [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/iokit/kiohiddeviceusagekey] and [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/iokit/kiohiddeviceusagepagekey] to the matching dictionary. # kIOHIDPrimaryUsagePageKey

**Macro**

A key that specifies the primary usage page of the device.

## Declaration

```swift
#define kIOHIDPrimaryUsagePageKey
```

## Discussion

The value of this key is the number for a HID usage page.

[doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/iokit/kiohidprimaryusagekey] and [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/iokit/kiohidprimaryusagepagekey] are not always rich enough to describe a device’s capabilities. Take, for example, a device that describes both a keyboard and a mouse in the same descriptor. To support this more complicated scenario, use the following keys instead:

- [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/iokit/kiohiddeviceusagekey]
- [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/iokit/kiohiddeviceusagepagekey]
- [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/iokit/kiohiddeviceusagepairskey]

To match on only one criterion, add [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/iokit/kiohiddeviceusagekey] and [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/iokit/kiohiddeviceusagepagekey] to the matching dictionary. To match on multiple criteria, add [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/iokit/kiohiddeviceusagepairskey] to the matching dictionary.





## Usage Keys

- **kIOHIDDeviceUsageKey**: A key that specifies a usage type of the device.
- **kIOHIDDeviceUsagePageKey**: A key that specifies a usage page of the device.
- **kIOHIDDeviceUsagePairsKey**: A key that contains the top-level usages of the device.
- **kIOHIDPrimaryUsageKey**: A key that specifies the primary usage type of the device.
